key_evidence: Massachusetts SJC ruling Commonwealth v. Meta Platforms, SJC-13747 (Apr 10, 2026): Section 230 does not shield Meta from Instagram design-features lawsuit. Court found Instagram design 'capitalizes on the developmental vulnerabilities of children' and that Meta was 'affirmatively misleading consumers about the safety of the Instagram platform.' Material EQU/ACC/INT downgrade anchor and in-window under 14-day recency rule.
New Mexico jury verdict (Mar 24, 2026): $375M civil penalties for consumer protection violations tied to child safety — Meta violated state consumer protection law by misleading users about safety.
Los Angeles jury verdict (Mar 25, 2026): Meta and YouTube found negligent in social-media addiction case, $3M award with Meta 70% responsible.

First full reassessment of Meta AI as a distinct ai-labs entry. Published 40.6 Functional pre-dates the 2026 verdict cluster. Proposed 29.4 Developing reflects the three-finding legal record and the design-feature-as-harm theory now validated by a state supreme court.
